-- We need to show that unreverse x has no reversible positions.
    -- We use the fact that if y is not a reversible move, then the reverseSet is {y}.
    -- This leads to a contradiction because unreverse x is constructed to bypass all reversible positions.
    intro h₅
    have h₆ : Reversible (unreverse x) (unreverse y) p := h₅
    have h₇ : p = left ∨ p = right := by cases p <;> simp
    cases p with
    | left =>
      -- For p = left, we derive a contradiction by showing that the reversible condition cannot hold.
      have h₈ : ∃ w ∈ (unreverse y).moves right, w ≤ unreverse x := h₆
      obtain ⟨w, hw, hw'⟩ := h₈
      have h₉ : w ≤ unreverse x := hw'
      have h₁₀ : w ∈ (unreverse y).moves right := hw
      have h₁₁ : Subposition w (unreverse y) := by
        apply Subposition.of_mem_moves
        exact h₁₀
      have h₁₂ : Subposition (unreverse y) (unreverse x) := by
        have h₁₃ : unreverse y ∈ x.unreverse.moves p := by
          apply mem_moves_unreverse_of_not_reversible hy h₁
        have h₁₄ : Subposition (unreverse y) (unreverse x) := by
          apply Subposition.of_mem_moves
          exact h₁₃
        exact h₁₄
      have h₁₅ : Subposition w (unreverse x) := by
        exact Subposition.trans h₁₁ h₁₂
      have h₁₆ : birthday w ≤ (unreverse x).birthday := by
        apply birthday_le_of_subposition h₁₅
      have h₁₇ : w ≤ unreverse x := hw'
      have h₁₈ : w.birthday < (unreverse x).birthday := by
        apply birthday_lt_of_subposition
        exact h₁₅
        <;> simp_all [unreverse_def]
        <;>
        (try contradiction) <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try linarith)
      have h₁₉ : w.birthday < w.birthday := by
        have h₂₀ : (unreverse x).birthday ≤ w.birthday := by
          apply birthday_le_of_le
          exact hw'
        linarith
      linarith
    | right =>
      -- For p = right, we derive a contradiction similarly.
      have h₈ : ∃ w ∈ (unreverse y).moves left, unreverse x ≤ w := h₆
      obtain ⟨w, hw, hw'⟩ := h₈
      have h₉ : unreverse x ≤ w := hw'
      have h₁₀ : w ∈ (unreverse y).moves left := hw
      have h₁₁ : Subposition w (unreverse y) := by
        apply Subposition.of_mem_moves
        exact h₁₀
      have h₁₂ : Subposition (unreverse y) (unreverse x) := by
        have h₁₃ : unreverse y ∈ x.unreverse.moves p := by
          apply mem_moves_unreverse_of_not_reversible hy h₁
        have h₁₄ : Subposition (unreverse y) (unreverse x) := by
          apply Subposition.of_mem_moves
          exact h₁₃
        exact h₁₄
      have h₁₅ : Subposition w (unreverse x) := by
        exact Subposition.trans h₁₁ h₁₂
      have h₁₆ : birthday w ≤ (unreverse x).birthday := by
        apply birthday_le_of_subposition h₁₅
      have h₁₇ : unreverse x ≤ w := hw'
      have h₁₈ : (unreverse x).birthday < w.birthday := by
        apply birthday_lt_of_subposition
        exact h₁₅
        <;> simp_all [unreverse_def]
        <;>
        (try contradiction) <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try linarith)
      have h₁₉ : (unreverse x).birthday < (unreverse x).birthday := by
        have h₂₀ : w.birthday ≤ (unreverse x).birthday := by
          apply birthday_le_of_le
          exact hw'
        linarith
